summary:
The maximum risk of ureter injury occurs during an abdominal hysterectomy due to its proximity to critical anatomical landmarks such as the uterine vessels; however, this procedure shows higher incidence rates than other methods despite lower overall risks associated with vaginally performed surgeries.
The primary source of high-risk ureter damage post-surgery lies within abdominopelvic procedures where close vicinity to major blood supply routes increases susceptibility significantly more so when agent1 (Round 2 summarized): The maximum risk of ureter injury occurs during an abdominal hysterectomy due to its proximity to critical anatomical landmarks such as the uterine vessels; however, this procedure shows higher incidence rates than other methods despite lower overall risks associated with vaginally performed surgeries.
The primary source of high-risk ureter damage post-surgery lies within abdominopelvic procedures where close vicinity to major blood supply routes increases susceptibility significantly more so when
